index.name: Machine translation
index.description: The use of software to translate text from one natural language to another.

index.name: Large language model
index.description: A neural network trained on vast amounts of text, capable of generating and translating language.

index.name: Project Gutenberg
index.description: A volunteer effort to digitize and archive public-domain books, the source of all Buchenberg texts.

index.name: Public domain
index.description: Creative works whose copyright has expired or never applied, free for anyone to use and distribute.

index.name: Back-translation
index.description: Translating text back to the source language to measure how much meaning was preserved.

index.name: Cosine similarity
index.description: A metric that measures the similarity between two vectors via the cosine of the angle between them.

index.name: Sentence embedding
index.description: A dense vector representation of a sentence that encodes its semantic meaning.

index.name: Ollama
index.description: An open-source runtime for running large language models locally and via hosted cloud models.

index.name: NLLB-200
index.description: Meta's open-source multilingual translation model supporting over 200 languages.

index.name: Mutation
index.description: The genetic operator that introduces variation to escape local optima — the lens behind self-refinement as anchored mutation.

index.name: X-ray style art
index.description: Prehistoric art depicting the bones and organs inside an animal, not just its skin — the attitude the project is named for.

index.name: Rock Art and the X-Ray Style
index.description: Joe Strummer's 1999 album — source of the line “I need to see in an x-ray style.”

about.name: Machine translation
about.description: The use of software to translate text from one natural language to another.

about.name: Large language model
about.description: A neural network trained on vast amounts of text, capable of generating and translating language.

about.name: Back-translation
about.description: Translating text back to the source language to measure how much meaning was preserved in the forward translation.

about.name: BLEU score
about.description: A metric for evaluating machine translation by measuring n-gram overlap with reference translations.

about.name: Cosine similarity
about.description: A metric that measures the similarity between two vectors by computing the cosine of the angle between them.

about.name: Sentence embedding
about.description: A dense vector representation of a sentence that encodes its semantic meaning in a high-dimensional space.

about.name: Transformer
about.description: A neural network architecture built on self-attention that forms the foundation of modern large language models.

about.name: NLLB-200
about.description: Meta's open-source multilingual translation model supporting over 200 languages.

about.name: Ludwig Wittgenstein
about.description: Philosopher whose shift from meaning-as-form to meaning-as-use underlies modern distributional NLP.

about.name: Project Gutenberg
about.description: A volunteer effort to digitize and archive public-domain books, the source of all Buchenberg texts.

about.name: Distributional semantics
about.description: The theory that a word's meaning is given by the contexts it appears in — the basis of embeddings.

about.name: word2vec
about.description: An early, influential method for learning word embeddings from large text corpora.

about.name: Attention
about.description: The mechanism weighing each token against all others, central to transformer translation.

about.name: Noam Chomsky
about.description: Linguist whose universal grammar shaped the rule-based era of machine translation.

about.name: ALPAC report
about.description: The 1966 report that halted early rule-based MT funding — a turning point in the field's history.

about.name: X-ray style art
about.description: Prehistoric art depicting the bones and organs inside an animal, not just its skin — the attitude the project is named for.

about.name: Rock Art and the X-Ray Style
about.description: Joe Strummer's 1999 album — source of the line “I need to see in an x-ray style.”

about.name: Claude
about.description: Anthropic's AI assistant — the working partner across Buchenberg's documented sessions.

geometry.name: Sentence embedding
geometry.description: A dense vector representation of a sentence that encodes its semantic meaning in a high-dimensional space.

geometry.name: Word embedding
geometry.description: A representation of words as vectors so that semantically similar words lie close together.

geometry.name: UMAP
geometry.description: A dimensionality reduction algorithm that projects high-dimensional data into 2D while preserving structure.

geometry.name: Dimensionality reduction
geometry.description: Techniques for reducing the number of dimensions of data while preserving meaningful structure.

geometry.name: Cosine similarity
geometry.description: A metric that measures the similarity between two vectors by computing the cosine of the angle between them.

geometry.name: Vector space model
geometry.description: A mathematical framework that represents text as numeric vectors in a high-dimensional space.

geometry.name: Attention
geometry.description: A mechanism that weighs the importance of each token relative to others, central to transformers.

geometry.name: The Library of Babel
geometry.description: Borges's story of a library of every possible book — meaning is worthless without an index.

geometry.name: Ludwig Wittgenstein
geometry.description: Philosopher whose 'meaning is use' and 'family resemblance' anticipate distributional semantics.

geometry.name: Jorge Luis Borges
geometry.description: Argentine writer whose fictions on libraries and copies prefigure problems of meaning and translation.

geometry.name: Family resemblance
geometry.description: Wittgenstein's idea that concepts share overlapping similarities, not a single essence — quantified by embeddings.

geometry.name: Pierre Menard, Author of the Quixote
geometry.description: Borges's story where an identical text means something new — the problem of translation, stated precisely.

art.name: Synesthesia
art.description: A neurological phenomenon in which one sense automatically triggers an experience in another.

art.name: Wassily Kandinsky
art.description: Russian abstract painter who theorized correspondences between color, form, music, and emotion.

art.name: Alexander Scriabin
art.description: Russian composer who theorized color-sound synesthesia and scored a keyboard of light.

art.name: Color organ
art.description: An instrument that maps sound to projected color — Scriabin's clavier à lumières.

art.name: Abstract art
art.description: Art that does not depict the visible world directly but works through form, color, and line.

art.name: Sonification
art.description: The use of non-speech audio to convey information — turning data into sound.

art.name: Pentatonic scale
art.description: A five-note musical scale; any sequence of its notes sounds consonant.

art.name: BLEU score
art.description: A metric for evaluating machine translation by measuring n-gram overlap with reference translations.

art.name: Sentence embedding
art.description: A dense vector representation of a sentence that encodes its semantic meaning — content before sense.

art.name: Jorge Luis Borges
art.description: Argentine writer whose fictions on copies and meaning belong to the project's intellectual lineage.

art.name: Flatland
art.description: Abbott's 1884 tale of a 2D world — the Square sees only a shadow of the sphere, as a sense under-samples meaning.

nlp.name: Named-entity recognition
nlp.description: An NLP task that locates and classifies named entities such as persons, organizations, and places.

nlp.name: spaCy
nlp.description: An open-source natural language processing library for Python, optimized for production use.

nlp.name: Co-occurrence
nlp.description: A statistical measure of how frequently two items appear together within a context window.

nlp.name: Knowledge graph
nlp.description: A network of entities and the relationships between them, represented as nodes and edges.

nlp.name: Relationship extraction
nlp.description: An NLP task that detects and classifies semantic relationships between entities in text.

nlp.name: Tokenization
nlp.description: Splitting text into smaller units (tokens) such as words or subwords for processing.

nlp.name: Tag cloud
nlp.description: A visual representation of word frequency where size reflects how often a term appears.

nlp.name: Force-directed graph
nlp.description: A graph drawing method that positions nodes using simulated physical forces.

nlp.name: Large language model
nlp.description: A neural network trained on vast text; Gemma4 normalizes entities in the NLP pipeline.

stats.name: Descriptive statistics
stats.description: Summary measures that describe and quantify features of a collection of data.

stats.name: Data visualization
stats.description: The graphical representation of data and information to aid understanding.

stats.name: Frequency distribution
stats.description: A summary of how often each value or category occurs in a dataset.

stats.name: Arithmetic mean
stats.description: The sum of values divided by their count — the average score per language or model.

stats.name: Cosine similarity
stats.description: The metric behind the translation scores aggregated on this page.

stats.name: BLEU score
stats.description: The classic n-gram translation metric, contrasted with Buchenberg's embedding scores.

stats.name: Sentence embedding
stats.description: The vector representation underlying every score shown here.

stats.name: Large language model
stats.description: The models whose winning rates are charted on this page.

stats.name: X-ray style art
stats.description: Prehistoric art depicting the bones and organs inside an animal, not just its skin — the attitude the project is named for.

stats.name: Rock Art and the X-Ray Style
stats.description: Joe Strummer's 1999 album — source of the line “I need to see in an x-ray style.”

learn.name: Language acquisition
learn.description: The process by which humans learn to perceive, produce, and use words to understand and communicate.

learn.name: Second-language acquisition
learn.description: The process of learning a language other than one's native tongue.

learn.name: Vocabulary
learn.description: The body of words known and used by a person in a given language.

learn.name: Gamification
learn.description: Applying game elements such as points and challenges to non-game contexts to boost engagement.

learn.name: Parallel text
learn.description: A text placed alongside its translation — the basis of every learning game here.

learn.name: Machine translation
learn.description: The software translation that produced the sentence pairs used in these games.

learn.name: Project Gutenberg
learn.description: The public-domain source of every sentence in the learning games.

learn.name: Flashcard
learn.description: A card bearing information on both sides, used in active recall practice.

reader.name: Parallel text
reader.description: A text placed alongside its translation, aligned for comparison — the heart of the Reader.

reader.name: Sentence embedding
reader.description: The vector representation behind the scores shown in X-Ray mode.

reader.name: Back-translation
reader.description: The round-trip translation whose score appears for each candidate in X-Ray mode.

reader.name: Cosine similarity
reader.description: The metric producing the translation and back scores per sentence.

reader.name: Machine translation
reader.description: The software translation displayed for each sentence.

reader.name: Large language model
reader.description: The models that generated the candidate translations you can inspect.

books.name: The Hound of the Baskervilles
books.description: Arthur Conan Doyle · 1902 — a Sherlock Holmes mystery.

books.name: The Big Four
books.description: Agatha Christie · 1927 — a Hercule Poirot novel.

books.name: Moby Dick
books.description: Herman Melville · 1851 — the hunt for the white whale.

books.name: Romeo and Juliet
books.description: William Shakespeare · c. 1597 — the tragedy of two lovers.

books.name: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
books.description: Lewis Carroll · 1865 — Alice down the rabbit hole.

books.name: The Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde
books.description: Robert Louis Stevenson · 1886 — a gothic novella.

books.name: Dracula
books.description: Bram Stoker · 1897 — the gothic vampire novel.

books.name: Flatland
books.description: Edwin A. Abbott · 1884 — a romance of many dimensions.
